generated_comment:
A: Arre yaar, aajkal har baat pe language policing shuru ho jaati hai. Hum log jo common gaali bol dete hain, uska matlab thodi na kisi ki maa-behen se hota hai.
B: Haan sir, bas expression hota hai.
A: Exactly. Aur kisi ladke ko bol diya ki itna mat ro, thoda strong bano, toh usme bhi issue bana dete hain. Itna sensitive hokar kaam nahi chalta.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This normalizes female-body/chastity-rooted abuse as 'just expression' and dismisses concerns about its misogynistic structure. It also encodes feminizing weakness ('mat ro, strong bano') as an insult, matching the frame's normalization and defensiveness.
original_frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
persona_text: You are Ajay. Age 38. A Rajput man working as a senior government officer in Jaipur. You are sitting in your office with the door closed talking to your junior male colleague after a female officer in your department submitted a formal request for work from home during her periods citing the new government circular. You are commenting on whether such requests should be entertained in a government office setting and what it means for discipline and productivity.
